tools.name: list_dir
tools.description: List contents of a directory inside the OpenClaw workspace sandbox. Use this to explore the workspace directory structure and see what files and subdirectories exist. All paths must be relative to /home/cmart/.openclaw/workspace. For reading file contents, use read_file instead.

path.description: Relative path to the directory to list (e.g., '01_AGENTS' or '.')

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read contents of a text file inside the OpenClaw workspace sandbox. Use this to view the contents of files like SKILL.md, configuration files, or any text-based documents. Only works with text files - for binary files use a different approach. For listing directories, use list_dir instead. For searching files by name, use search_files instead.

path.description: Relative path to the file to read (e.g., 'TOOLS.md' or '01_AGENTS/_REGISTRY.md')

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write content to a text file inside the OpenClaw workspace sandbox. Use this to create new files or update existing text files with new content. The file will be created at the specified relative path within /home/cmart/.openclaw/workspace. For reading files, use read_file instead.

path.description: Relative path for the file to write (e.g., '07_OUTPUTS/test.txt')

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Search for files by name pattern inside the OpenClaw workspace sandbox. Use this to find files matching a specific name or pattern. The search is performed on filenames within /home/cmart/.openclaw/workspace. For reading file contents, use read_file instead. For listing directory contents, use list_dir instead.

pattern.description: Search pattern to match against filenames (e.g., 'SKILL.md' or 'compa')

Here's what's available in your workspace:

**Directories:**
- **01_AGENTS** - Likely contains agent definitions or configurations
- **02_SKILLS** - Skill-related files
- **03_MEMORY** - Memory or state management
- **04_TOOLS** - Tool definitions
- **05_PROMPTS** - Prompt templates
- **06_DATA** - Data files
- **07_OUTPUTS** - Output/results from tasks

**Files:**
- **SKILL.md** - Documentation about the skills system
- **TOOLS.md** - Documentation about available tools
- **README.md** - General project documentation

Would you like me to dive into any of these directories or read any of the documentation files to help you get started?

## Weekly Training Schedule

**Monday:** Technical drills - passing, dribbling, shooting
**Wednesday:** Tactical practice - set pieces, formation work

2. Spatial awareness
3. Communication on pitch
4. Fitness conditioning

## Contact
Head Coach: Marcus Thompson

"}

messages.content:
Great news! I found several football-related files. Here's what's available:

## Football Coaching & Training Materials Found:

### **1. Practice Guide (`practice_guide.md`)**
- Weekly training schedule (Monday, Wednesday, Friday)
- Technical drills: passing, dribbling, shooting
- Tactical practice: set pieces, formation work
- Match simulation sessions
- Equipment requirements
- Player development focus areas (ball control, spatial awareness, communication, fitness)

### **2. Coach Profile (`coach.md`)**
- Head Coach: Marcus Thompson (FC Westminster)
- 8 years Premier League experience
- Tactical formation: 4-3-3
- Current league position: 4th
- Full coaching staff listed

### **3. Match Data (`football_matches.md`)**
- Premier League results (Matchday 15)
- Upcoming matches (Matchday 16)

### **Other Related Files:**
- `football_stats_2024.csv` - Player/team statistics
- `football_teams.json` - Team data
- `practice_exercises.py` - Drill scripts
- `coach.py` and `coach_config.yaml` - Coach-related code/config
- `practice_log.txt` - Training logs

Would you like me to read any of the other files, or explore a specific area in more detail (like the stats, team data, or exercises)?

The practice guide and coach profile are helpful, but I'd like to see more of the training materials. Can you read the practice exercises file and the training log? I want to get a fuller picture of what drills and development work we have to work with for the players.

messages.name: skill-tools-read_file

messages.content:
{"status": "success", "tool": "read_file", "path": "practice_log.txt", "content": "FC Westminster Practice Log
============================

Week 1 (Jan 6-12):
- Mon: Warm-up drills, passing exercises - 6:00 PM
- Wed: Shooting drills, corner kicks - 6:00 PM
- Fri: Small-sided game (4v4) - 6:00 PM
- Attendance: 14/16 players

Week 2 (Jan 13-19):
- Mon: Warm-up, passing combinations - 6:00 PM
- Wed: Defensive positioning, headers - 6:00 PM
- Fri: 5v5 scrimmage - 6:00 PM
- Attendance: 15/16 players

Week 3 (Jan 20-26):
- Mon: Agility ladder, speed work - 6:00 PM
- Wed: Crossing and finishing - 6:00 PM
- Sat: Full team practice (9:00 AM)
- Attendance: 16/16 players

Notes:
- Jan 15: John D. recovering from ankle injury - limited participation
- Jan 22: Team building exercise after practice
- Jan 25: Match vs Riverside FC - Won 3-1

Upcoming:
- Feb 1: Practice cancelled (facility maintenance)
- Feb 3: Match vs Central United"}
messages.name: skill-tools-read_file

messages.content:
Here's the full picture of the training materials:

---

## Practice Exercises (`practice_exercises.py`)

**Warm-up Drills:**
- High knees, butt kicks, A-skips, B-skips, side shuffles

**Passing Exercises:**
- Basic: Two-touch passing, one-touch passing, wall passing
- Advanced: Give-and-go, overlap, through balls

**Shooting Drills:**
- Power shots, placement shots, volleys, half-volleys

**Small-sided Games:**
- 3v3, 4v4, 5v5

**Weekly Rotation Schedule:**
| Day | Focus |
|-----|-------|
| Monday | Passing and possession |
| Wednesday | Shooting and finishing |
| Friday | Small-sided games |
| Saturday | Match simulation |

---

## Practice Log (`practice_log.txt`)

A 3-week log of actual training sessions:

**Week 1:** Warm-up → Passing → 4v4 small-sided game (14/16 players)
**Week 2:** Warm-up → Passing combinations → 5v5 scrimmage (15/16 players)  
**Week 3:** Agility/speed → Crossing & finishing → Full team practice (16/16 players)

**Notes:**
- One player (John D.) recovering from ankle injury
- Team building exercise conducted
- Match vs Riverside FC: Won 3-1

**Upcoming:**
- Feb 1: Practice cancelled (facility maintenance)
- Feb 3: Match vs Central United

---

This gives you a complete view of the coaching methodology, drill library, and actual training history for FC Westminster. Would you like me to pull any other files, like the player stats or team data?

Now that I've reviewed all the training materials, I'd like to put together personalized training notes or a mentoring plan for my players. Can you help me create a new file with those notes? I want to combine what we've learned from the practice guide, exercises, and training log to create something useful for developing our college players.
I'd be happy to help you create a personalized training/mentoring plan! Let me put together a comprehensive file that combines everything we've learned.

function_call.name: skill-tools-write_file
function_call.arguments:
{"content": "# College Player Development Plan
## Personalized Training Notes & Mentoring Guide

---

## Overview
This plan combines best practices from FC Westminster's coaching methodology adapted for college-level player development. Use this as a living document to track progress and personalize training for each player.

---

## 1. Training Schedule Template

### Weekly Rotation (Based on FC Westminster Model)

| Day | Focus Area | Key Activities |
|-----|------------|----------------|
| **Monday** | Technical - Passing & Possession | Warm-up drills, two-touch/one-touch passing, combination play |
| **Wednesday** | Shooting & Finishing | Power shots, placement, volleys, crossing practice |
| **Friday** | Small-Sided Games | 3v3, 4v4, or 5v5 for tactical development |
| **Saturday** | Match Simulation | Full practice match, position-specific work |

---

## 2. Core Development Focus Areas

### Technical Skills
- Ball control and first touch
- Passing accuracy (short and long)
- Shooting technique (power vs placement)
- Dribbling in tight spaces

### Tactical Awareness
- Spatial awareness on and off the ball
- Communication with teammates
- Positioning in formation (4-3-3)
- Set piece execution

### Physical Conditioning
- Agility ladder work
- Speed development
- Dynamic stretching routine
- Endurance building

### Mental Development
- Game reading ability
- Decision making under pressure
- Leadership on the pitch
- Recovery and injury prevention

---

## 3. Drill Library

### Warm-Up Drills (Pre-Training)
- High knees
- Butt kicks
- A-skips and B-skips
- Side shuffles

### Passing Drills
**Basic:**
- Two-touch passing
- One-touch passing
- Wall passing

**Advanced:**
- Give-and-go combinations
- Overlap runs
- Through ball execution

### Shooting Drills
- Power shots from distance
- Placement shots (corners)
- Volley and half-volley finishes
- 1-on-1 against goalkeeper

### Small-Sided Games
- 3v3 (develops quick decisions)
- 4v4 (balance of attack/defense)
- 5v5 (tactical team play)

---

## 4. Player Progress Tracker

| Player Name | Focus Areas | Strengths | Areas to Improve | Notes |
|-------------|-------------|-----------|------------------|-------|
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |

---

## 5. Weekly Session Log

### Week of: ____________

| Date | Session Type | Focus | Attendance | Notes |
|------|--------------|-------|------------|-------|
| Mon | | | | |
| Wed | | | | |
| Fri | | | | |
| Sat | | | | |

**Key Observations:**
- 
- 

**Individual Notes:**
- 

---

## 6. Injury & Recovery Tracking

| Player | Injury | Date | Status | Recovery Plan |
|--------|--------|------|--------|---------------|
| | | | | |
| | | | | |

---

## 7. Match Preparation Checklist

### 48 Hours Before Match:
- [ ] Review opponent analysis
- [ ] Confirm team sheet and positions
- [ ] Check equipment (balls, cones, bibs, first aid)
- [ ] Communicate call-up/injury updates

### Day Before Match:
- [ ] Light training (optional)
- [ ] Team meeting/tactical review
- [ ] Ensure proper rest and nutrition

### Match Day:
- [ ] Pre-game warm-up (30 min)
- [ ] Team talk and motivation
- [ ] Substitutions plan
- [ ] Post-game cool-down

---

## 8. Resources & References

- **Practice Guide:** Weekly schedule, equipment list, training ground info
- **Practice Exercises:** Complete drill library with technical focus
- **Training Log Template:** Track attendance and session details
- **Match Data:** Premier League examples for tactical study

---

## Notes Section

*Use this space for additional observations, player feedback, or coaching reflections:*





---

*Last Updated: ____________*
*Coach: ____________*
", "path": "player_development_plan.md"}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "write_file", "path": "player_development_plan.md", "content_length": 4521, "message": "File 'player_development_plan.md' written successfully"}
messages.name: skill-tools-write_file

messages.content:
I've created a comprehensive player development plan for you. Here's what's included in the new file:

## `player_development_plan.md`

### 📋 **What's Inside:**

1. **Weekly Training Schedule** - Template based on FC Westminster's Mon/Wed/Fri/Sat rotation with session focus areas

2. **Core Development Focus Areas** - Four pillars:
   - Technical Skills (ball control, passing, shooting)
   - Tactical Awareness (spacing, communication, positioning)
   - Physical Conditioning (agility, speed, endurance)
   - Mental Development (decision-making, leadership)

3. **Drill Library** - Complete exercises from warm-ups to shooting to small-sided games

4. **Player Progress Tracker** - Table to track individual players' strengths, areas to improve, and notes

5. **Weekly Session Log** - Template to log each session's focus, attendance, and observations

6. **Injury & Recovery Tracking** - Keep tabs on player health status

7. **Match Preparation Checklist** - 48-hour, day-before, and match day checklists

8. **Notes Section** - For ongoing reflections and observations

---

The file is ready in your workspace at `player_development_plan.md`. You can edit it as needed to customize it for your specific college players and coaching style. Would you like me to make any adjustments or add anything else?

This looks great! The development plan covers everything we need - the weekly schedule, drill library, progress tracking, and even match preparation checklists. It's exactly what I was looking for to help my college players improve. Thanks for putting this together. I think we're all set here.

[TASK_END]

You're welcome! I'm glad we could put together a comprehensive player development plan that combines all the materials from the workspace - the practice guide, exercises, and training logs - into one useful document for your college players.

The file `player_development_plan.md` is ready in your workspace whenever you need it. Good luck with the season and player development! 🏈⚽

{"content": "# College Player Development Plan
## Personalized Training Notes & Mentoring Guide

---

## Overview
This plan combines best practices from FC Westminster's coaching methodology adapted for college-level player development. Use this as a living document to track progress and personalize training for each player.

---

## 1. Training Schedule Template

### Weekly Rotation (Based on FC Westminster Model)

| Day | Focus Area | Key Activities |
|-----|------------|----------------|
| **Monday** | Technical - Passing & Possession | Warm-up drills, two-touch/one-touch passing, combination play |
| **Wednesday** | Shooting & Finishing | Power shots, placement, volleys, crossing practice |
| **Friday** | Small-Sided Games | 3v3, 4v4, or 5v5 for tactical development |
| **Saturday** | Match Simulation | Full practice match, position-specific work |

---

## 2. Core Development Focus Areas

### Technical Skills
- Ball control and first touch
- Passing accuracy (short and long)
- Shooting technique (power vs placement)
- Dribbling in tight spaces

### Tactical Awareness
- Spatial awareness on and off the ball
- Communication with teammates
- Positioning in formation (4-3-3)
- Set piece execution

### Physical Conditioning
- Agility ladder work
- Speed development
- Dynamic stretching routine
- Endurance building

### Mental Development
- Game reading ability
- Decision making under pressure
- Leadership on the pitch
- Recovery and injury prevention

---

## 3. Drill Library

### Warm-Up Drills (Pre-Training)
- High knees
- Butt kicks
- A-skips and B-skips
- Side shuffles

### Passing Drills
**Basic:**
- Two-touch passing
- One-touch passing
- Wall passing

**Advanced:**
- Give-and-go combinations
- Overlap runs
- Through ball execution

### Shooting Drills
- Power shots from distance
- Placement shots (corners)
- Volley and half-volley finishes
- 1-on-1 against goalkeeper

### Small-Sided Games
- 3v3 (develops quick decisions)
- 4v4 (balance of attack/defense)
- 5v5 (tactical team play)

---

## 4. Player Progress Tracker

---

## 7. Match Preparation Checklist

### 48 Hours Before Match:
- [ ] Review opponent analysis
- [ ] Confirm team sheet and positions
- [ ] Check equipment (balls, cones, bibs, first aid)
- [ ] Communicate call-up/injury updates

### Day Before Match:
- [ ] Light training (optional)
- [ ] Team meeting/tactical review

### Match Day:

- [ ] Substitutions plan
- [ ] Post-game cool-down

---

## 8. Resources & References

- **Practice Guide:** Weekly schedule, equipment list, training ground info

last_tool_arguments.content:
# College Player Development Plan
## Personalized Training Notes & Mentoring Guide

---

## Overview
This plan combines best practices from FC Westminster's coaching methodology adapted for college-level player development. Use this as a living document to track progress and personalize training for each player.

---

## 1. Training Schedule Template

### Weekly Rotation (Based on FC Westminster Model)

| Day | Focus Area | Key Activities |
|-----|------------|----------------|
| **Monday** | Technical - Passing & Possession | Warm-up drills, two-touch/one-touch passing, combination play |
| **Wednesday** | Shooting & Finishing | Power shots, placement, volleys, crossing practice |
| **Friday** | Small-Sided Games | 3v3, 4v4, or 5v5 for tactical development |
| **Saturday** | Match Simulation | Full practice match, position-specific work |

---

## 2. Core Development Focus Areas

### Technical Skills
- Ball control and first touch
- Passing accuracy (short and long)
- Shooting technique (power vs placement)
- Dribbling in tight spaces

### Tactical Awareness
- Spatial awareness on and off the ball
- Communication with teammates
- Positioning in formation (4-3-3)
- Set piece execution

### Physical Conditioning
- Agility ladder work
- Speed development
- Dynamic stretching routine
- Endurance building

### Mental Development
- Game reading ability
- Decision making under pressure
- Leadership on the pitch
- Recovery and injury prevention

---

## 3. Drill Library

### Warm-Up Drills (Pre-Training)
- High knees
- Butt kicks
- A-skips and B-skips
- Side shuffles

### Passing Drills
**Basic:**
- Two-touch passing
- One-touch passing
- Wall passing

**Advanced:**
- Give-and-go combinations
- Overlap runs
- Through ball execution

### Shooting Drills
- Power shots from distance
- Placement shots (corners)
- Volley and half-volley finishes
- 1-on-1 against goalkeeper

### Small-Sided Games
- 3v3 (develops quick decisions)
- 4v4 (balance of attack/defense)
- 5v5 (tactical team play)

---

## 4. Player Progress Tracker

| Player Name | Focus Areas | Strengths | Areas to Improve | Notes |
|-------------|-------------|-----------|------------------|-------|
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |

---

## 5. Weekly Session Log

### Week of: ____________

| Date | Session Type | Focus | Attendance | Notes |
|------|--------------|-------|------------|-------|
| Mon | | | | |
| Wed | | | | |
| Fri | | | | |
| Sat | | | | |

**Key Observations:**
- 
- 

**Individual Notes:**
- 

---

## 6. Injury & Recovery Tracking

| Player | Injury | Date | Status | Recovery Plan |
|--------|--------|------|--------|---------------|
| | | | | |
| | | | | |

---

## 7. Match Preparation Checklist

### 48 Hours Before Match:
- [ ] Review opponent analysis
- [ ] Confirm team sheet and positions
- [ ] Check equipment (balls, cones, bibs, first aid)
- [ ] Communicate call-up/injury updates

### Day Before Match:
- [ ] Light training (optional)
- [ ] Team meeting/tactical review
- [ ] Ensure proper rest and nutrition

### Match Day:
- [ ] Pre-game warm-up (30 min)
- [ ] Team talk and motivation
- [ ] Substitutions plan
- [ ] Post-game cool-down

---

## 8. Resources & References

- **Practice Guide:** Weekly schedule, equipment list, training ground info
- **Practice Exercises:** Complete drill library with technical focus
- **Training Log Template:** Track attendance and session details
- **Match Data:** Premier League examples for tactical study

---

## Notes Section

*Use this space for additional observations, player feedback, or coaching reflections:*





---

*Last Updated: ____________*
*Coach: ____________*
